data.title: FCPT acquires Patient First Urgent Care property in PA for $6.6M, part of its net-leased properties portfolio.
data.description: Located in Mill Valley, California, Four Corners Property Trust (NYSE: FCPT) recently acquired a Patient First Urgent Care property for $6.6 million. This real estate investment trust specializes in owning and acquiring top-notch, net-leased restaurant and retail properties. The purchased property is situated in a bustling retail area in Pennsylvania and is under a corporate-operated long-term triple net lease. This acquisition expands FCPT's portfolio and strengthens its presence in the commercial real estate market.

data.title: Football exec and charity founder Jim Murray, 87, passed away. He co-led Eagles to 1981 Super Bowl, and co-founded the first Ronald McDonald House.
data.description: Prior to his role in guiding the Philadelphia Eagles to the Super Bowl in 1981, he played a key part in establishing the inaugural Ronald McDonald House to support families of critically ill children.

data.title: Trump intensifies pressure on chip companies with tariff threats after securing deals with Nvidia and AMD, keeping tech world on edge.
data.description: The latest move by President Donald Trump involves increasing pressure on chip companies through a remarkable tariff threat. This action comes after successfully negotiating deals with notable companies such as Nvidia and Advanced Micro Devices.

data.title: $100M raised by startups for programmable money, coinciding with stablecoin market cap hitting $280B.
data.description: On the day when the total market capitalization of stablecoins hit $280 billion, two startups, M0 and Rain, disclosed securing almost $100 million in fresh funding rounds to advance their programmable money initiatives.
